texto: Here was a man who'd learned to write before he could think, a man who threw out logical fallacies like tacks behind a getaway car, and he always always always got away.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Helen DeWitt: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Here was a man who'd learned to write before he could think, a man who threw out logical fallacie…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, men, always pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

texto: The reason is that even in a fantasy there is nothing even remotely erotic about a toilet bowl. In fact, considered as an accoutrement to a sexual encounter, a toilet bowl is a real cold shower.
explicacao: Citação de Helen DeWitt sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “The reason is that even in a fantasy there is nothing even remotely erotic about a toilet bowl. I…” articula reflexao, sex, toilets em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como toilets.

texto: When you play a piece of music there are so many different ways you could play it. You keep asking yourself what if. You try this and you say what if and you try that. When you buy a CD you get one answer to the question. You never get the what if.
explicacao: Citação de Helen DeWitt sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “When you play a piece of music there are so many different ways you could play it. You keep askin…” articula reflexao, music, piece em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.
